prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion model, assuming stock prices follow a log-normal distribution. Historical daily log returns for PG over the last 5 years were analyzed to estimate the daily drift (mean log return, approximately 0.000414) and daily volatility (standard deviation of log returns, approximately 0.010550). These parameters were then scaled for a 21-day period to calculate the expected future log price and its standard deviation. The point estimate represents the median of the predicted log-normal price distribution. Confidence intervals are constructed using the Z-scores corresponding to the desired confidence levels, assuming the log returns are normally distributed. This model assumes future price movements will reflect past trends and volatility, which may not hold true due to market shifts or company-specific news.
